perturbation.original_text: Each of Acquiror and SPI hereby represents and warrants to the Company that the statements set forth in Schedule 3.02 are true and correct as of the date of this Agreement and will be true and correct as of the Launch Date and the Closing Date (or, if made as of a specified date, as of such specified date only).
perturbation.changed_text: Each of Acquiror and SPI hereby represents and warrants to the Company that the statements set forth in Schedule 3.02 are believed to be true and correct as of the date of this Agreement and will be believed to be true and correct as of the Launch Date and the Closing Date (or, if made as of a specified date, as of such specified date only).
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'are true and correct' to 'are believed to be true and correct' introduces ambiguity regarding the standard of accuracy required for the representations and warranties. The original wording implies a strict standard of accuracy, while the altered text suggests a lower standard based on belief, undermining the enforceability of these representations and warranties. This contradiction affects Section 5.02, where Acquiror's indemnity is tied to any inaccuracy of a representation or warranty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Acquiror shall indemnify the Company from and against all Losses incurred by the Company to the extent arising out of or resulting from (i) any inaccuracy or breach of a representation or warranty made by Acquiror under Section 3.02 or (ii) any breach or failure by Acquiror to perform any of its covenants or obligations contained in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the standard of accuracy required for representations and warranties in Schedule 3.02 from 'are true and correct' to 'are believed to be true and correct'. This introduces a significant ambiguity, potentially undermining the enforceability of those representations. This directly contradicts the requirement in the contradicted text (Section 5.02) that Acquiror indemnify the Company for 'any inaccuracy or breach of a representation or warranty made by Acquiror under Section 3.02'. Because the standard has been lowered, it creates conflict with how Section 5.02 would be enforced. The change means that Acquiror could argue they believed the representation was true, even if later proven factually incorrect, thus avoiding the indemnity obligation.

perturbation.original_text: Following the Closing Date, SPI shall develop an integration plan in consultation with the management of the Company as required for combining the business operations of the two companies. Subject to the goals, parameters and integration activities outlined in the integration plan, SPI shall (i) provide the employees of the Company with employee incentives under such terms and conditions as not less favorable (taking into account, among other things, tax implications) to the incentives made available by the Company to such its employees as of the date of this Agreement […***…], and thereafter under such terms and conditions as not less favorable (taking into account, among other things, tax implications, local laws, and SPI's practices with respect to the employees of Acquiror) to those of the incentives made available by SPI to its employees, and (ii) […***…] the […***…] of the […***… ] of the […***…] as of the […***…] of this [… ***…] at […***…] the […***…] of the […***…] of the […***…] on […***…]. For the sake of achieving the purpose of the strategic alliance as set forth in Section 2.01, both Parties acknowledge their mutual intention to, in principle, maintain the Company's employment at levels consistent with the requirements of the Company from time to time.
perturbation.changed_text: Following the Closing Date, SPI may develop an integration plan with or without consulting the management of the Company as it deems necessary for potentially combining the business operations of the two companies. Subject to any goals, parameters and integration activities that SPI wishes to adhere to or not adhere to as outlined in any integration plan, SPI may, but is not obligated to, (i) provide the employees of the Company with something vaguely resembling employee incentives under vaguely defined terms and conditions that may or may not be less favorable (not taking into account, among other things, tax implications) to any vague incentives hypothetically made available by the Company to some of its employees sometime around the date of this Agreement […***…], and thereafter under such terms and conditions as it deems best (not taking into account, among other things, tax implications, local laws, and SPI's practices with respect to the employees of Acquiror) to anything that it vaguely labels as incentives made available by SPI to its employees, and (ii) […***…] the […***…] of the […***… ] of the […***…] as of the […***…] of this [… ***…] at […***…] the […***…] of the […***…] of the […***…] on […***…]. For the sake of hypothetically achieving some vague approximation of the purpose of the strategic alliance as set forth in Section 2.01, both Parties acknowledge their somewhat conflicting intentions to, in principle, maybe consider maintaining the Company's employment at what may or may not be levels consistent with some vague notion of the requirements of the Company from time to time.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing words like 'shall' with 'may,' 'required' with 'necessary,' and adding phrases like 'or without consulting,' 'vaguely resembling,' and 'what may or may not be levels consistent,' the modified text introduces significant uncertainty and optionality into SPI's obligations regarding the integration plan and employee incentives. This contradicts the original intent to ensure a smooth integration and maintain employee incentives, creating ambiguity regarding the terms and conditions of employment following the acquisition. It weakens the assurances given to the Company's employees and undermines the strategic alliance's purpose stated in Section 2.01.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For the purpose of creating a combined company that can drive considerable growth in global markets, including Japan, the Parties agree to form a strategic business alliance (the "Strategic Business Alliance") among Acquiror and SPI and its Affiliates (collectively, the "Sucampo Group") and the Company, subject to the successful Closing. The Parties intend to achieve the purpose of such Strategic Business Alliance by mutual cooperation in, among others, the following areas: (a) ensuring that the transaction contemplated in this Agreement would provide Sucampo Group with increased revenues—primarily from combining Sucampo Group's existing sales with those from the Company—enhanced profitability, strong cash flow generation and a robust balance sheet and the improved financial strength of SPI as the parent company would also accrue to the benefit of its subsidiaries, which will include the Company after the Closing; (b) ensuring that Sucampo Group and the Company together would have a deeper and broader development pipeline of potential drug candidates in development across four major therapeutic areas—gastroenterology, ophthalmology, autoimmune, and inflammation—and greater resources, both financially and operationally, to maximize these opportunities, and consistent with the Target Company's business strategy, such development pipelines (some of these drug candidates) could be out-licensed to external firms to create even greater value; and (c) ensuring that both Sucampo Group and the Company would have greater opportunity to conduct business development transactions, and through the relationships of the Company and increased presence in the Japanese market, Sucampo Group would gain greater access to the Japanese biotech community and Japan's well-regarded scientific institutions and researchers, and the Company would receive access to Sucampo Group's expertise in identifying, negotiating and managing key alliances.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly states SPI 'shall' develop a plan and provide incentives, while the changed text uses 'may' and introduces uncertainty ('with or without consulting,' 'vaguely resembling,' 'may or may not be levels consistent'). This directly contradicts the contradicted section's emphasis on 'ensuring' increased revenues, enhanced profitability, and a deeper development pipeline through the strategic alliance. The changed text weakens the commitment to these goals, creating a clear and direct conflict with the intended purpose of the alliance. The altered text makes SPI's actions optional, undermining the original assurance of a strong and mutually beneficial partnership, thus fulfilling the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: SPI hereby absolutely, unconditionally and irrevocably guarantees to and in favor of the Company that the Acquiror shall perform and discharge any and all of its obligations under this Agreement as set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: SPI hereby attempts to provide some form of limited guarantee to and vaguely in favor of the Company that the Acquiror will vaguely attempt to perform and only partially discharge maybe some of its assumed obligations under this Agreement as loosely interpreted within this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing the definitive language ('absolutely, unconditionally, irrevocably guarantees') with weaker, ambiguous terms ('attempts to provide some form of limited guarantee', 'vaguely attempt to perform', 'partially discharge maybe some of its assumed obligations') significantly undermines the strength and reliability of the guarantee provided by SPI. This creates a direct conflict with the original, absolute guarantee, making it unclear to what extent SPI is actually bound to ensure Acquiror's performance. If Acquiror fails to fulfill its obligations, the Company's ability to seek recourse from SPI is severely compromised due to the altered wording.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SPI hereby attempts to provide some form of limited guarantee to and vaguely in favor of the Company that the Acquiror will vaguely attempt to perform and only partially discharge maybe some of its assumed obligations under this Agreement as loosely interpreted within this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text provides an 'absolutely, unconditionally and irrevocably' guarantee. The changed text significantly weakens this guarantee by using ambiguous and limited terms such as 'attempts to provide some form of limited guarantee', 'vaguely attempt to perform', and 'partially discharge maybe some of its assumed obligations.' This directly contradicts the original guarantee, creating uncertainty about the extent of SPI's responsibility. The fact that the changed text is the same as the contradicted text only reinforces the fact that a change was introduced that creates a direct conflict of meaning as the original meaning was an 'absolute guarantee'. The 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition explains that in-text contradictions arise when terms are used interchangeably without definition, leading to conflicting obligations. By changing definitive language to vague language, such a conflict has been created. This falls under misaligned terminology because the nature of the obligation has been modified by creating ambiguity where there was none before. Therefore, a contradiction exists.
